topic: AI Coding Acceleration Whiplash: Why More AI Means More Bugs

competitors.title: AI Code Generation Benchmarks 2026
key_points: AI-generated PRs wait 4.6x longer in code review without governance frameworks
AI coding assistants introduce 15-18% more security vulnerabilities in PRs without oversight
Teams using AI generate code 40-60% faster but face increased bug rates
Review tax: time saved writing is often re-spent auditing AI output

competitors.title: AI Coding Assistant Statistics 2026
key_points: Developer trust in AI tools dropped from 70%+ in 2023 to just 29% in 2025
84% adoption but only 29% trust rate creates a 'trust gap'
Code churn increased from 3.1% to 5.7% with AI assistance
METR trial showed experienced developers 19% slower on complex tasks

competitors.title: The AI Productivity Paradox: Individual Gains vs. Team Slowdowns
key_points: METR 2025 RCT found a 19% net slowdown for experienced developers on complex tasks despite perceived speedups
Review bottlenecks erase gains from faster code generation
Verification tax creates hidden costs in AI adoption
Organizational metrics (DORA) often don't improve despite individual developer time savings

competitors.title: Measuring AI ROI Using DORA + SPACE Metrics
key_points: Measurement accuracy gap: 39-44% between perceived and actual productivity
Warns against using LOC, raw PR counts, or commits as business KPIs
Focus on delivery metrics (deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR) vs raw productivity metrics
AI acts as amplifier: magnifies strengths AND dysfunctions of existing teams

stats.fact: AI coding assistants introduce 15-18% more security vulnerabilities in PRs without oversight
Developer trust in AI tools dropped from 70%+ in 2023 to just 29% in 2025
METR 2025 RCT found a 19% net slowdown for experienced developers on complex tasks despite perceived speedups
stats.source: METR randomized controlled trial 2025 / faros.ai analysis

stats.fact: AI-generated PRs wait 4.6x longer in code review without governance frameworks
Code churn increased from 3.1% to 5.7% with AI assistance
Measurement accuracy gap: 39-44% between perceived and actual productivity
Organizations with high AI adoption saw PR cycle times drop 24%, from 16.7 to137. hours median
90% of enterprise engineering teams now use AI somewhere in the development lifecycle
Only 5% of enterprises achieve real measurable financial returns from AI tools in 2026
AI generates 41-46% of all code globally in 2026, up from ~26% in 2023

angles: The 'acceleration whiplash': how faster code generation creates a downstream bug surge
Trust collapse (70% → 29%): why developers don't trust AI output despite widespread adoption
The verification tax paradox: time saved writing code is re-spent auditing it
The productivity measurement gap: why developers feel 20% faster but measure 19% slower
Governance vs. acceleration: how to structure AI workflows to avoid quality degradation
Code churn economics: 5.7% vs 3.1% and what that means for long-term tech debt
The experienced developer slowdown: why AI hurts top performers on complex tasks
From copilots to liability: when AI tools create more work than they save
keywords: why AI tools create more bugs
